commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces two empty `failDelivery` rejection catches in outbound delivery cleanup with warning logs and adds regression tests for both rejection paths.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main has two empty `failDelivery` rejection handlers at `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:1298` and `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:1328`, and the existing outbound test harness can force `failDelivery` to reject on both paths.
commands.review_followup: No automated source repair is indicated; merge should wait for contributor real behavior proof or an explicit maintainer validation/override of the proof gate.

target.title: fix(delivery): log failDelivery errors instead of silently swallowing
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Two `failDelivery()` call sites in the outbound delivery pipeline use `.catch(() => {})`, silently swallowing errors when recording a delivery as failed in the queue.
- Why it matters: If `failDelivery` rejects (e.g., database connection lost), the delivery queue entry is left in an inconsistent state with no diagnostic trace. The adjacent `ackDelivery()` handler at lines 1308-1316 properly logs warnings on failure, making this an inconsistency in error handling.
- What changed: Replaced both empty `.catch(() => {})` handlers with `.catch((err) => { log.warn(...) })` that includes the queue ID and formatted error message, matching the existing `ackDelivery` pattern. Added two regression tests covering both rejection paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to delivery logic, queue management, retry behavior, or the `ackDelivery` handler. Only error reporting in the two `failDelivery` catch blocks.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83113
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `failDelivery()` errors silently swallowed by `.catch(() => {})`, leaving no diagnostic trace when queue state updates fail.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux (Grok Build), Node, openclaw main at 1b1580cbc3 with fix cherry-picked.
- **Exact steps or command run**:
  1. Cherry-picked fix onto `origin/main`, installed deps with `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
  2. **GREEN pass** — ran tests with the production fix applied:
     ```bash
     node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
     ```
  3. **RED pass** — reverted only `deliver.ts` to unfixed `origin/main` (keeping new tests), re-ran:
     ```bash
     git checkout HEAD -- src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts
     node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
     ```
  4. **Restore** — re-applied production fix, confirmed GREEN again.

- **Evidence after fix (GREEN — 85/85 pass)**:
  ```
  ✓ |infra| ../../src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts (85 tests) 2218ms

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  85 passed (85)
    Start at  07:59:42
    Duration  12.25s (transform 9.68s, setup 1.50s, import 7.41s, tests 2.22s, environment 0ms)
  ```

- **Evidence without fix (RED — 2 new tests fail, 83 pre-existing pass)**:
  ```
  ❯ |infra| ../../src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts (85 tests | 2 failed) 1178ms
     × logs a warning when failDelivery rejects on bestEffort partial failure (#83113) 9ms
     × logs a warning when failDelivery rejects in the error handler (#83113) 3ms

  FAIL  |infra| deliver.test.ts > deliverOutboundPayloads > logs a warning when failDelivery rejects on bestEffort partial failure (#83113)
  Error: expected warn call #1
   ❯ requireMockCall ../../src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts:156:11

  FAIL  |infra| deliver.test.ts > deliverOutboundPayloads > logs a warning when failDelivery rejects in the error handler (#83113)
  Error: expected warn call #1
   ❯ requireMockCall ../../src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts:156:11

  Test Files  1 failed (1)
       Tests  2 failed | 83 passed (85)
    Start at  08:02:18
    Duration  4.23s (transform 2.42s, setup 350ms, import 2.43s, tests 1.18s, environment 0ms)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: With the fix applied, `failDelivery` rejection triggers `log.warn` with queue ID and error message. Without the fix, `.catch(() => {})` silently discards the rejection — `log.warn` is never called, causing both new tests to fail with `expected warn call #1`.
- **What was not tested**: Live database connection failure during delivery. The proof exercises the real `deliver.ts` code path with injected queue/logger dependencies, not a production instance. Red-green verification confirms the `.catch()` handlers deterministically fire.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The `.catch(() => {})` pattern was used as a fire-and-forget safety net, but omitted any logging. The adjacent `ackDelivery()` call at lines 1308-1316 already follows the correct pattern with `log.warn()`, making this an oversight rather than an intentional design choice.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No lint rule catches empty catch handlers with intentionally discarded errors.
- Contributing context (if known): The two `failDelivery` calls were likely added at different times; the pattern was copied from the (correct) `ackDelivery` call but the logging was omitted.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: When `failDelivery` rejects, `log.warn` is called with the queue ID and underlying error message. Two tests added, one per call site:
  1. `logs a warning when failDelivery rejects on bestEffort partial failure (#83113)` — covers the partial-failure path at line ~1298.
  2. `logs a warning when failDelivery rejects in the error handler (#83113)` — covers the outer error-handler path at line ~1334.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Each test mocks `failDelivery` to reject and asserts the warning log contains the queue ID and error message. No other assertions or side effects.
- Red-green verified: Tests fail deterministically without the production fix (`.catch(() => {})` never calls `log.warn`), pass with it.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This only adds warning-level log output when an internal queue state update fails, which was previously silent.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Grok Build)
- Runtime/container: Node
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Default delivery config

### Steps

1. Mock `failDelivery` to reject (simulating queue storage failure)
2. Run `deliverOutboundPayloads` through the bestEffort partial-failure path or the outer error-handler path
3. Assert `log.warn` was called with queue ID and underlying error

### Expected

- `log.warn` message with queue ID and error details

### Actual

- Before: No log output, error silently discarded
- After: Warning logged with queue ID and formatted error message

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Red-green terminal output (deterministic proof)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Red-green verified both `failDelivery` rejection paths. GREEN: 85/85 tests pass with fix. RED: exact 2 new tests fail without fix (`expected warn call #1`). Confirmed `.catch()` handlers deterministically fire when `failDelivery` rejects.
- Edge cases checked: Verified that successful `failDelivery` calls (no rejection) are unchanged (83 pre-existing tests pass in both GREEN and RED runs).
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end delivery failure with a real database outage. Proof exercises the real deliver.ts logic with injected dependencies.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

None. The change is purely additive logging in an existing error-handling path with no control flow impact.
